zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots於1274年7月11日出生在蘇格蘭費伏時，她的父親Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster15歲，母親Margaret Guines15歲。她於1302年與Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots在英國艾可萊特爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1327年10月27日在蘇格蘭班福爾卡倫享年53歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林登福姆林 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots于1274年7月11日出生在苏格兰費伏时，她的父亲Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster15岁，母亲Margaret Guines15岁。她于1302年与Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots在英国艾可萊特爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1327年10月27日在苏格兰班福爾卡倫享年53岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰費伏登福姆林登福姆林 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots was born on 11 July 1274, in Fife, Scotland, her father, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, was 15 and her mother, Margaret Guines, was 15. She married Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots in 1302, in Writtle, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 27 October 1327, in Cullen, Banffshire, Scotland, at the age of 53, and was buried in Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots est née le 11 juillet 1274, à Fife, Écosse, son père, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Margaret Guines, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots en 1302, à Writtle, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 27 octobre 1327, à Cullen, Banffshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterrée à Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat 1302 in Writtle, Essex, England Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 27. it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots è nata il il 11 luglio 1274 nel Fife, Scotland, sua padre, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, aveva 15 anni e sua madre, Margaret Guines, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots nel 1302, nel Writtle, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scotsが1274年7月11日にスコットランドフィフで生まれた時，父Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulsterは15歳，母Margaret Guinesは15歳であった。1302年にイギリスエセックスウリットルでRobert I the Bruce, King of Scotsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1327年10月27日にスコットランドバンフシュアイアクウレンで53歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインダンフエルムライン アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots(이)가 1274년 7월 11일 스컷랜드 피프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Guines(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1302년 영국 에세스 리틀에서 Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1327년 10월 27일 스컷랜드 반프실르 컬렌에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 던퍼라이인 던퍼라이인 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots nasceu em 11 de julho de 1274, em Fife, Scotland, seu pai, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Guines, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots em 1302, em Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 27 de outubro de 1327, em Cullen, Banffshire, Scotland, com 53 anos, e foi sepultada em Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Sc: дунфирмлаин эбби, дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.at Athassel Priory, near Cashel, County: атасселабби, коунти оф т кросс оф типпирэри, Ирландия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots родилась 11 июля 1274 г. в местности фиф, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Guines, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots в 1302 г. в местности риттл, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 27 октября 1327 г. в местности куллен, банффшир, Шотландия в возрасте 53 лет и была похоронена в местности дунфирмлаин эбби, дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth de Burgh, Queen of Scots nació el 11 de julio de 1274, en Fife, Scotland, su papá, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Margaret Guines, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots en 1302, en Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 27 de octubre de 1327, en Cullen, Banffshire, Scotland, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultada en Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido.
